---
title: "Ladybug PopGen -- Project History"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

**Last Updated:** 2026-04-13

**Working Project Name:** Ladybug PopGen (BIOL624 Final Project)

**Purpose:** This document is the chronological record of the Ladybug PopGen project's design decisions and development work. It contains the Decision Register (all significant methodological, architectural, and design decisions with full rationale) and the Development Log (chronological record of substantive work sessions). Together, these sections provide a complete, traceable history of how the project reached its current state.

**Relationship to other documents:** This is one of two tiers of project documentation:

- **Master doc** (`ladybug_master_doc.Rmd`): Current-state reference describing what the project is and how it is designed. Architecture, component summaries, methodology, results, limitations, references.
- **This document** (`ladybug_project_history.Rmd`): Chronological record of how the project got to its current state. Decision Register and Development Log.

---

# 1. Decision Register

Significant methodological, architectural, and design decisions logged in a consistent format. Newest entries at the bottom (chronological order).

### 2025-11-17 -- Script Standardization

**Decision:** Adopt a comprehensive script header format for all analysis scripts, including title, project, author, contact, dates, purpose, inputs, outputs, and usage examples.

**Rationale:** Ensures reproducibility and makes scripts self-documenting. Every script should be usable by someone who has never seen the project before.

**Alternatives considered:** Minimal headers (rejected: insufficient for reproducibility). README-only documentation (rejected: separates docs from code).

**Impact:** All scripts (diversity.sh, fst.sh, comprehensive_selection_scan.R, etc.) follow this format.

### 2025-11-20 -- 5kb Window Size for All Windowed Analyses

**Decision:** Use 5,000 bp non-overlapping windows for Tajima's D, nucleotide diversity, and windowed FST calculations.

**Rationale:** Tested 1kb, 5kb, and 10kb windows. 1kb had insufficient SNPs per window for reliable statistics. 10kb had poor spatial resolution. 5kb was optimal for our SNP density (~80,000 SNPs per chromosome). Using the same window size across all windowed analyses enables direct comparison between metrics.

**Alternatives considered:** 1kb (rejected: too few SNPs per window), 10kb (rejected: low spatial resolution), overlapping windows (rejected: added complexity without clear benefit for our questions).

**Impact:** All windowed analyses use consistent 5kb windows. Selection scan outlier windows are directly comparable to diversity metrics.

### 2025-11-20 -- Median for Population Summary Statistics

**Decision:** Report median (not mean) values for Tajima's D and pi population summaries.

**Rationale:** Per-window distributions are right-skewed. Median is more robust to outliers and better represents the "typical" window.

**Impact:** Summary tables in master doc report median values.

### 2025-12-06 -- Dual-Method Selection Scan (SUPERSEDED)

**Decision:** Use OutFLANK for low-FST comparisons (among-invasive) and percentile thresholds for high-FST comparisons (CHI vs invasive).

**Rationale:** OutFLANK failed on CHI vs invasive comparisons due to high genome-wide differentiation (windowed FST 0.05-0.06 at the time). Lotterhos & Whitlock (2014) confirmed OutFLANK performance degrades when mean FST exceeds 0.05-0.10.

**Alternatives considered:** OutFLANK-only (rejected: zero outliers for CHI vs invasive). BayeScan (rejected: similar demographic sensitivity issues). Percentile-only (initially rejected for lack of statistical rigor, later adopted -- see 2026-01-09).

**Impact:** Led to development of pattern-based biological categorization (BOTH / INVASION_SPECIFIC / POST_INVASION). **Superseded by unified percentile approach on 2026-01-09.**

### 2025-12-06 -- Pattern-Based Biological Categorization

**Decision:** Classify outlier SNPs/windows into three biological categories based on which comparison types flag them: BOTH (CHI-vs-invasive AND among-invasive), INVASION_SPECIFIC (CHI-vs-invasive only), POST_INVASION (among-invasive only).

**Rationale:** The invasion study design creates a natural framework for distinguishing selective processes. Among-invasive comparisons share the bottleneck history, so outliers in those comparisons reflect post-invasion local adaptation with minimal demographic confounding. CHI-vs-invasive comparisons mix demographic and selective effects.

**Alternatives considered:** No categorization (rejected: loses biological information from the study design).

**Impact:** Central to biological interpretation of all selection scan results. Retained across the method revision from dual-method to unified percentile.

### 2025-12-08 -- Two-Step BLAST Annotation Pipeline

**Decision:** Annotate selection candidate genes using a two-step BLAST cascade: C. septempunctata -> H. axyridis (>=50% identity, E-value <=1e-10) -> T. castaneum (>=40% identity, E-value <=1e-10).

**Rationale:** Direct NCBI queries yielded only 56% annotation coverage for C. septempunctata genes. H. axyridis is the closest sequenced relative (same family, ~20-30 MY divergence). T. castaneum is the best-annotated beetle genome (model organism for Coleoptera). The two-step cascade achieves 100% gene mapping.

**Alternatives considered:** Direct NCBI only (rejected: 56% coverage insufficient). Single-step BLAST to T. castaneum (rejected: loses the higher-confidence H. axyridis annotations). InterProScan (considered for future use, not yet implemented).

**Impact:** All 1,844 selection candidates annotated. Functional categorization and GO enrichment depend on this pipeline.

### 2026-01-09 -- Dual FST Calculation (Genome-Wide + Windowed)

**Decision:** Modify fst.sh to calculate both genome-wide FST (no windowing, for population comparisons) and windowed FST (5kb windows, for selection scans) in a single run.

**Rationale:** Original analysis reported only windowed FST means (0.05-0.06 for CHI vs invasive), which were 4-5x lower than classmate values and literature expectations (0.15-0.25). Root cause: windowed means are dominated by the ~95% neutral windows with low FST, while genome-wide weighted FST weights by heterozygosity and better captures overall differentiation. Neither approach is wrong -- they answer different questions.

**Alternatives considered:** Switching entirely to genome-wide FST (rejected: lose the windowed distribution needed for selection scans). Keeping only windowed FST (rejected: can't compare with literature or classmates).

**Impact:** Genome-wide weighted FST (0.14-0.15 for CHI vs invasive) now aligns with literature. Windowed FST distribution retained for selection scan input. All prior selection scan results remain valid.

### 2026-01-09 -- Unified Percentile Approach with OutFLANK Validation

**Decision:** Use the unified percentile method (top 0.5%, 1%, 2% of FST distribution) as the PRIMARY selection detection method for ALL six pairwise comparisons. OutFLANK serves as VALIDATION for among-invasive comparisons only.

**Rationale:** OutFLANK diagnostic re-analysis confirmed: (a) excellent statistical fit (df=2) for all comparisons, but (b) zero outliers for CHI vs invasive because FSTbar (~0.11-0.12) approaches observed FST (~0.15-0.17), treating the entire demographic signal as neutral background. Unified percentile provides methodological consistency, transparent thresholds, and no need to justify method-switching between comparisons.

**Alternatives considered:** Dual-method approach (superseded: inconsistent methodology harder to justify in publications). OutFLANK-only (rejected: fails for CHI vs invasive). BayeScan (rejected: similar limitations).

**Impact:** Replaces the December 2025 dual-method approach. 1,963 outlier windows at 1% threshold. Pattern categorization retained. OutFLANK concordance validation completed (3-4%, reflecting methodological differences not failure).

### 2026-01-12 -- pcadapt Selected Over FLK/hapFLK

**Decision:** Use pcadapt (not FLK/hapFLK) as the third selection detection method.

**Rationale:** (1) Multiple introductions are likely, violating FLK's tree-like divergence assumption. (2) pcadapt handles reticulate population history naturally (no tree needed). (3) Small CHI sample size (n=5) is problematic for FLK's outgroup requirement. (4) With only 4 populations, FLK has limited degrees of freedom. (5) pcadapt is computationally lighter (15-20 min vs hours for hapFLK).

**Alternatives considered:** FLK/hapFLK (rejected: tree assumption violated, small outgroup, limited df). BayeScan (rejected: similar issues to OutFLANK for high-FST comparisons).

**Impact:** pcadapt analysis in progress. PC interpretation framework: PC1 outliers -> INVASION_SPECIFIC, PC2 outliers -> POST_INVASION, multiple PCs -> BOTH.

### 2026-01-12 -- Three-Tier BLAST Cascade Design

**Decision:** Upgrade annotation pipeline to three tiers by adding D. melanogaster (>=30% identity, E-value <=1e-5) as a safety net for genes unresolved by H. axyridis and T. castaneum.

**Rationale:** D. melanogaster has the best-annotated insect genome (decades of FlyBase curation). Phylogenetically more distant (~300-350 MY) but provides coverage for highly conserved genes that may lack beetle-specific annotations.

**Alternatives considered:** Two-tier only (rejected: may miss conserved genes with poor beetle annotation). DIAMOND instead of BLAST (still under consideration for speed). InterProScan (still under consideration for GO enrichment).

**Impact:** Implementation deferred until selection scan validation is complete. Identity thresholds and confidence scoring documented in master doc Section 3.4.

### 2026-01-12 -- SNP-Level Concordance for Cross-Method Validation

**Decision:** Disaggregate percentile outlier windows to individual SNPs for concordance calculation with OutFLANK and pcadapt.

**Rationale:** Previous window-level concordance (3-4%) may reflect aggregation artifacts. Percentile identifies high-average-FST windows while OutFLANK identifies extreme individual SNPs. These can disagree at window level even if detecting the same underlying signal. SNP-level comparison removes this scale mismatch.

**Alternatives considered:** Window-level only (rejected: known aggregation artifact risk).

**Impact:** All SNPs within percentile outlier windows flagged as "percentile outliers" for SNP-level comparison.

### 2026-01-12 -- Remove Unverifiable 66% Cross-Validation Claim

**Decision:** Remove the claim that "66% of outliers were detected in >=3 independent population comparisons" from all documentation.

**Rationale:** The source of this statistic could not be verified. It may have been from an earlier analysis iteration with different parameters. Keeping unverifiable claims undermines the documentation's credibility.

**Impact:** Updated cross-method validation statistics documented in master doc Section 3.3.

### 2026-01-13 -- fst.sh Script Refactoring

**Decision:** Refactor fst.sh from 851 lines to 165 lines, removing all summary generation, statistical functions, and biological interpretations. Create separate summarize_fst.R for reporting.

**Rationale:** Biological interpretations in fst.sh (FST thresholds, "HIGH/LOW" labels, expected patterns) were added by Claude based on general population genetics conventions, not cited literature. User requested removal to keep scripts focused on computation only, with reporting separated into R.

**Alternatives considered:** Keep interpretations but add citations (rejected: user preference for clean separation of computation and interpretation).

**Impact:** fst.sh now produces raw FST files + simple genome-wide table. summarize_fst.R generates cumulative reports with statistics only.

---

# 2. Development Log

Chronological record of substantive work sessions. Newest entries at the bottom (chronological order).

### 2025-11-17 -- Project Initiation

**Work performed:** Established script standardization. Conducted preliminary VCF file analysis: confirmed 10 chromosomes (NC_058189.1 through NC_058198.1) with ~80,000 SNPs each, 88 total samples across 4 populations.

**Outputs:** Standardized script header template. Initial data characterization.

**Next steps identified:** Begin diversity analysis.

### 2025-11-17 to 2025-11-20 -- Diversity Analysis Development

**Work performed:** Developed diversity.sh for Tajima's D and nucleotide diversity (pi) using vcftools. Implemented 5kb non-overlapping windows. Ran population-specific analyses for CHI, EEU, WEU, USA, and combined. Extended to site-level pi calculations. Implemented sex chromosome handling.

**Outputs:** Per-chromosome Tajima's D and pi files. Summary reports. Key finding: clear demographic hierarchy CHI > EEU > WEU > USA, with 12-14% diversity reduction in invasive populations.

**Next steps identified:** FST analysis, selection scans.

### 2025-12-06 -- Selection Scan Development

**Work performed:** Developed comprehensive_selection_scan.R. Discovered OutFLANK failure on high-FST comparisons. Implemented dual-method approach (OutFLANK for low-FST, percentile for high-FST). Created pattern-based categorization system (BOTH / INVASION_SPECIFIC / POST_INVASION). Implemented cross-threshold validation (0.5%, 1%, 2%).

**Outputs:** Selection scan results. Pattern categorization. Cross-threshold validation.

**Next steps identified:** Finalize diversity analysis. Complete FST analysis. Gene annotation.

### 2025-12-08 -- Analysis Completion (Phase 1-2)

**Work performed:** Finalized diversity analysis with detailed summary reports. Completed FST analysis across all population pairs. Discovered windowed-vs-genome-wide FST discrepancy with classmates. Implemented two-step BLAST annotation pipeline. Completed functional categorization of 1,844 selection candidates.

**Outputs:** Final diversity tables. FST results (windowed only at this point). Gene annotation (100% mapping via two-step BLAST). Functional categorization: signal transduction (26%), transport (24%), protein processing (18%), transcriptional regulation (12%).

**Next steps identified:** Resolve FST methodology question. Consider additional validation methods.

### 2026-01-09 -- FST Methodology Revision and OutFLANK Diagnostics

**Work performed:** Diagnosed windowed-vs-genome-wide FST discrepancy (root cause: windowed means dominated by neutral regions). Modified fst.sh to compute both approaches. Ran genome-wide FST on all 6 comparisons -- confirmed 0.14-0.15 for CHI vs invasive, matching literature. Ran OutFLANK diagnostic on all 6 comparisons -- confirmed df=2 (excellent fit) but zero outliers for CHI vs invasive (FSTbar ~0.11-0.12 approaches observed FST). Created unified_selection_scan.R applying percentile method to all 6 comparisons. Generated 66 FST plots.

**Outputs:** Genome-wide FST values (see master doc Section 3.2.3). OutFLANK diagnostic results (see master doc Section 3.3.2). Unified percentile results: 1,963 outlier windows at 1% threshold (BOTH: 18, INVASION_SPECIFIC: 431, POST_INVASION: 845). Files in `results/outflank_diagnostic/` and `results/unified_selection_scan/`.

**Next steps identified:** Calculate OutFLANK-percentile concordance (blocked by SNP ID format mismatch). Consider pcadapt or FLK for additional validation.

### 2026-01-12 -- Concordance Validation and Annotation Planning

**Work performed:** Fixed SNP ID format mismatch in outflank_diagnostic.R (changed from generic SNP_# to CHROM:POS). Created calculate_outflank_concordance.R. Ran concordance: EEU vs WEU 3.11% (39 windows), EEU vs USA 3.87% (12 windows). Interpreted low concordance as reflecting methodological differences, not failure. Evaluated FLK vs pcadapt -- selected pcadapt. Confirmed three-tier BLAST cascade design. Removed unverifiable 66% cross-validation claim.

**Outputs:** Concordance results in `results/outflank_diagnostic/concordance/`. Updated documentation. pcadapt task specification for Claude Code.

**Next steps identified:** Run pcadapt. Implement three-tier BLAST (deferred until selection scan validation complete).

### 2026-01-13 -- PLINK Conversion, Sample QC, Script Refactoring

**Work performed:** Completed PLINK conversion from per-chromosome filtered VCFs (with `--set-missing-var-ids '@:#'` fix for variant ID collisions). Validated sample missingness: discovered 11 individuals >20% missing (not just the 2 initially flagged). CHI effective n ~ 3.3 due to CHN11 (60% missing). Refactored fst.sh (851 -> 165 lines) and created summarize_fst.R.

**Outputs:** PLINK files at `data/plink/ladybug_snps.bed/bim/fam` (803,821 variants x 88 individuals, 87.4% genotyping rate). Sample missingness report. Refactored fst.sh and new summarize_fst.R.

**Next steps identified:** User decision on sample exclusion (blocking pcadapt). If excluding, regenerate PLINK files and potentially re-run earlier analyses.

**Technical notes for future reference:**

- pcadapt `type='pool'` is for pooled sequencing data (allele frequencies), not individual genotypes. Use `type='bed'` with PLINK files.
- PLINK merge fails when VCF variant IDs are all `.` because PLINK uses variant ID as primary key. Fix: `--set-missing-var-ids '@:#'` assigns CHROM:POS as unique IDs.
- WG_VARIANTS VCF is UNFILTERED. Must use per-chromosome filtered VCFs from FILTERED_CHR_VCFS.
- Conda in non-interactive bash: `source /Users/reina/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh && conda activate pop_gen`

---

# 3. Instructions for Claude

## 3.1 Core Editing Rules

**This document is append-only.** Content in Sections 1 (Decision Register) and 2 (Development Log) is never deleted or modified except to fix factual errors. The full history of the project's development reasoning must remain intact and traceable.

When updating this document, preserve all existing information and details. New entries are appended to the appropriate section.

## 3.2 What Claude Should Always Do

- **Date everything.** Every log entry, decision, and significant update gets a date.
- **Document the "why."** Never record a methodological or design choice without its rationale. If the rationale is uncertain, say so explicitly.
- **Flag uncertainty.** Use language like "tentative," "requires validation," or "assuming X holds" rather than presenting uncertain conclusions as settled.
- **Flag follow-ups** with a **[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]** tag so they are not lost between sessions.
- **State limitations proactively** when recording design decisions.
- **Maintain consistency.** If a decision recorded here contradicts content in the master doc, surface the contradiction and update the relevant documents.
- **Cross-reference appropriately.** Decision Register entries that affect specific pipeline components should reference the relevant master doc section. Development Log entries should reference the Decision Register entries from the same session.

## 3.3 What Claude Should Never Do

- **Never silently overwrite content.** Changes and their reasons must be visible.
- **Never fabricate citations or technical details.** Flag uncertainty rather than stating guesses as fact.
- **Never omit caveats.** This is an internal development record, not marketing material.
- **Never assume context not present in this document.** Ask rather than guess.

## 3.4 Tone and Voice

Direct, precise, academic style. No filler, no fluff, no motivational framing. The goal is a record the developer can trust as accurate and complete.
